title: traditional baseline repair for P04f anomaly strata
primary_metric: held-out duplicate-readout charge res68_abs_frac; lower is better
finding: Raw selected-pulse reproduction passes exactly (640,737 vs 640,737). The held-out winner is strong_huber_baseline_repair with res68 0.0110 and run-block CI [0.008671793390406786, 0.013669986675151999]; the best traditional baseline-repair comparator is strong_huber_baseline_repair with res68 0.0110. P09a baseline-excursion and early-pretrigger rows remain measurable stress strata under run/stave/amplitude/peak/saturation matching, so the safest interpretation is a duplicate-readout electronics repair benchmark rather than external energy recovery.

next_tickets.title: Baseline-repair external calibration-pulse audit for S16g P04f closure
next_tickets.body: Question: do S16g baseline-repair residual improvements on P09a baseline-excursion and early-pretrigger strata survive when the repair features are calibrated on independent calibration or forced/random-like pretrigger windows rather than same-event beam pretrigger samples? Expected information gain: separates genuine pedestal correction from same-waveform duplicate-readout electronics closure without duplicating the queued pseudo-pedestal live-time and charge-harm tickets.
